questions.question: If a qubit is in the state |0⟩, what is the expectation value of the Pauli-Z operator (⟨Z⟩)?

explanations.a: 0 indicates an equal mix of states.
explanations.b: An expectation value of ⟨Z⟩ = 1 signifies the qubit is completely in the |0⟩ state.
explanations.c: -1 means the qubit is in the |1⟩ state.
explanations.d: 0.5 is not a possible result for state |0⟩ under measurement Z.

questions.question: What does an expectation value of ⟨Z⟩ = 0 imply about the qubit state?

answers.c: The qubit has equal probabilities of collapsing to |0⟩ or |1⟩ upon measurement.
answers.d: The qubit is in an invalid state.

explanations.c: When the expectation value is 0, it means the average result is midway between +1 and -1, implying equal probabilities.
explanations.d: The state is valid, but measurement results are balanced.

questions.question: Which Pauli matrix corresponds to the observable represented by [[0, 1], [1, 0]]?

explanations.a: The matrix representation for Pauli-Z is [[1, 0], [0, -1]].
explanations.b: The matrix representation for Pauli-Y involves complex numbers.
explanations.c: The matrix representation for Pauli-X is exactly [[0, 1], [1, 0]].

questions.question: In quantum mechanics, what does the expectation value ⟨A⟩ signify?

answers.a: The result of a single measurement.
answers.b: The average value expected after many measurements on identically prepared systems.
answers.c: The maximum possible value that can be observed.
answers.d: The probability of the qubit collapsing.

explanations.a: A single measurement always yields one of the eigenvalues, not the expectation value (unless the state is an eigenstate).
explanations.b: Expectation value is defined as the statistical average of measurement outcomes.
explanations.c: The maximum possible value is simply the largest eigenvalue.
explanations.d: The expectation value is a mean, not a probability itself.

questions.question: The Pauli-X operator measures the projection of the qubit spin along which axis of the Bloch sphere?

explanations.a: Pauli-Z measures alignment along the Z-axis.
explanations.b: Pauli-Y measures alignment along the Y-axis.
explanations.c: Pauli-X measures whether the qubit is aligned along the X-axis (states |+⟩ and |-⟩).
explanations.d: Each Pauli operator is dedicated to a specific principal axis.
